rulings.Case Title: EST. OF DONALD EUGENE CHERRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required for counsel to explain the significant delay in finalizing the settlement. The draft settlement was initially reported to the Court 4 months ago at the 05/19/2025 hearing. At the last hearing on 06/17/2025, the administrator's counsel reported to the Court that counsel had not even discussed the settlement with her client-administrator (see minutes of the 06/17/2025 hearing). The judicial officer is to review and determine whether to grant an additional continuance and keep both petitions on calendar, while waiting for a petition to approve the settlement to be filed and assigned a new hearing date, or whether to consider proceeding to: 1) Issue an order to appear and show cause why the administrator should not be suspended or removed for having filed nothing since December 2005, after taking 50% fees ($6,382); 2) Take the petition to suspend/remove off calendar as moot, in favor of the Court's issuance of the order to appear and show cause on its own motion to dispense with formal service of a citation on the administrator otherwise required by Probate Code section 8500(b); and/or 3) Appoint Karen Lawrence as successor general administrator and grant her petition as prayed if the Court decides to remove, rather than suspend, the administrator, and set a status hearing on the filing of a final account by the administrator within 60 days, as required by section 10952, or appoint Karen Lawrence as special administrator with general powers while keeping her petition for appointment as successor general administrator on calendar if the Court decides to suspend, rather than remove, the administrator. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL SALOMON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/2/25. Examiner's Notes with the following issues sent to attorney on 8/12/25: 1) The petition has not been verified by the petitioner. 2) The bank statement certification language is deficient. 3) Although referenced in the caption, the petition itself does not indicate what the end of account date is. If the end of account date will be 6/30/25, an additional statement for Chase 833 that includes the 6/30/25 date will need to be filed. 4) Petitioner alleges that $1,394 total were disbursed mistakenly but that the estate has since been paid back, but after the accounting period. The $1,394 must be included as assets on hand, but notated as post-account receipts.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROOSEVELT THOMPSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 04/09/2025). The petition has been pendng for more than 4 months since it was initially set for 04/16/2025: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form (notice of hearing with proof of service) is on file. 3) Paragraph 3 of petition: Has petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? 4) Paragraph 10: The decedent's bank accounts that existed at the time of his death as reported by the decedent's conservator in his 8th interim account must also be listed on the inventory and appraisal. 5) Paragraph 12: Waivers of account must also be signed by Keisha Smith, Kendal Slocum, Khrishna Moore, and Katema Slocum as issue of a predeceased child of the decedent and additional heirs. 6) Paragraph 15: The allegation re investment of cash is incomplete. 7) Paragraph 21: The statutory fees must be recalculated after the inventory and appraisal is corrected to add the decedent's bank accounts. 8) Paragraph 24: The settlement agreement made in October 2024 is silent as to the decedent's property subject to the omnibus clause. Absent additional agreement among the heirs, each heir is entitled to his or her statutory share (either 1/3 or 1/12). 9) Paragraph 25: According to the October 2024 settlement agreement, Roosevelt Thompson Jr. will not receive any interest in the estate real property. Keisha Smith, Kendal Slocum, Khrishna Moore, and Katema Slocum (whose names and addresses were not set forth in this paragraph) will each receive 1/8. 10) The estate was to receive $175,134.92 from the decedent's conservator per the conservatorship termination order issued in January 2024. Please explain why the estate has no cash on hand. 11) The attorneys' statutory fees must be ordered to be paid out of the estate, as required by Probate Code section 10831(d). Distributees must advance sufficient cash to the estate to create the required source of payment, or the attorneys must consent to a lien (with or without 5% simple annual interest available under LRSF 14.56) on the estate real property sought to be distributed in kind. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF GEORGINA LAXMI LOREK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/15/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The petition was only cursorily reviewed online, as no courtesy copy was provided. The attorney fee declaration does not appear to include time sheets. (4) The report accompanying each account must contain a statement of the age, health, and whereabouts of the ward(s). [LRSF 14.46 D.1.] (5) Does the report address sufficiency of bond? Please provide courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/1/25. Issues should not be "answered" in a declaration numbering these issues: a proof of service of the complete Notice of Hearing should just be filed; bank statement(s) should just be filed; and additional information should just be provided in a verified supplement to the petition or the attorney fee declaration should just be supplemented by the attorney with the necessary time records. (The fiduciary is a guardian, not an administrator.) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTOR BUI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/23/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney with the following issues: 1) No allegation has been made re: Decedent's income taxes, fiduciary income taxes, and federal estate taxes. 2) Par 3, which discusses a revocation of waiver of account, appears to be regarding a different estate. Please clarify. 3) Par 13 is incomplete. Please allege why the Director of the California Victim Compensation Board does not need to be given notice, using the exact language in Probate Code 9202(b). 4) Par 14 states that the Franchise Tax Board will be given notice prior to the final hearing; however, notice needed to have been sent not later than 90 days after the date letters were issued to the administrator. Please provide the notice required therein, allege that notice was given pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c), except that it was not within 90 days of issuance of letters, and provide the exact date that the notice was given. 5) No allegation has been made regarding notice under Probate Code 9202(a). 6) Please allege whether all reasonably ascertainable creditors have been given notice. 7) Proposed order has not been filed. 8) Notice of Hearing has not been given. 9) Waivers of account have not been filed as indicated would be in par. 17.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAYDEN CHEUKHEI KAM
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Although the petition signed on 6/9/25 requested 90 days['] extension be granted without specifying 90 days from which date, the administrator will be given the benefit of the doubt and be granted 90 days' extension of time to administer the estate from the date of this order, not the date he signed the petition. Status of filing a petition for final distribution or a second report of status with a petition requesting additional time to administer the estate will be set for 11/18/25. No appearance will be required on that date if a petition is filed by 11/4/25.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF IMOGENE B. GIELING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/15/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Both mailed and published notice must clearly indicate that the will is lost. [LRSF 14.20.] No proof of service or proof of publication of notice has been filed yet, showing that the petitioner seeks to admit a LOST will. (2) Questions about the heirs and beneficiaries listed in the petition and on Exhibit A to the third supplement filed on 6/18/25 still have not been addressed. Is Elizabeth Frey, who is identified as the daughter of Herbert Albert, different from Elizabeth Frey, who is identified as Decedent's daughter and the mother of great-nephews (?) Julian Redman Frey and Jeremy Chance Griffin? Please provide a copy of the lost will to be attached to the revised proposed order [LRSF 14.20] and courtesy copies of the any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/1/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/01/2025, as the following issue remains unaddressed: If there is no personal representative of the settlor's estate, the settlor's heirs who are not beneficiaries of the trust (Todd McMechen and Misty Duhagon) must be served in the manner required by CCP sections 413.10 et seq. See Probate Code sections 851(b)(3) & 17203(b). Mere mailing is not a manner of service authorized by the CCP provisions (applicable to service of civil summons).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RONALD JOSEPH FRENETTE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing Status of closing the estate will be set for 2/23/2027. The administrator must file either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. C. Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 2//23/2027. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PING JEE YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court to determine whether the void marking on the will is an act constituting revocation pursuant to Probate Code Section 6120(b). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
